code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about a sports team (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language templates (full sentence)
    templates = {
        "numberOfMembers": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o} members",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"{s} played in the {o} season",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o} league",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays at {o}",
        "fullName": lambda s, o: f"The full name of {s} is {o}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"The chairman of {s} is {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"The owner of {s} is {o}",
        "nickname": lambda s, o: f"{s} is nicknamed {o}",
        "chairmanTitle": lambda s, o: f"The {o} of {s}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "part": lambda s, o: f"{s} includes {o}",
        "mayor": lambda s, o: f"The mayor of {s} is {o}",
        "champions": lambda s, o: f"The champions of {s} are {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The language of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda s, o: f"The leader party of {s} is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "youthclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} was in youth club {o}",
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{s} is operated by {o}",
        "tenant": lambda s, o: f"{s} is tenant of {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the city of {o}",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the state of {o}",
    }

    # Build clauses, merging simple subject‑object chains into relative clauses
    clauses: list[str] = []
    used = set()  # indices of triples already merged as secondary clauses
    for i, t in enumerate(triples):
        if i in used:
            continue
        formatter = templates.get(t.predicate)
        base_clause = formatter(t.subject, t.object) if formatter else f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}"

        # Look ahead for a triple whose subject equals the current object
        merged = False
        for j in range(i + 1, len(triples)):
            if j in used:
                continue
            u = triples[j]
            if u.subject == t.object:
                formatter2 = templates.get(u.predicate)
                # Build a relative clause starting with "who"
                rel_clause = (
                    formatter2("who", u.object)
                    if formatter2
                    else f"who {u.predicate} {u.object}"
                )
                base_clause = f"{base_clause}, {rel_clause}"
                used.add(j)          # mark the secondary triple as consumed
                merged = True
                break
        clauses.append(base_clause)

    # Combine clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence_body = clauses[0]
    else:
        sentence_body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]

    # Ensure the sentence ends with a period
    sentence = sentence_body.strip()
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: SportsTeam. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:

(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)

"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."
